name: OmopDiagnosticReportCodedView
title: DiagnosticReport → measurement / observation / procedure_occurrence (FHIR-flat, stage 1; per-coding fan-out)
description:
Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP. One staging row per `DiagnosticReport.code.coding[]` entry (per-coding fan-out) instead of one row per report. This single canonical staging table (staging.diagnosticreport_coded) feeds all three domain-routed sibling edges (measurement / observation / procedure_occurrence).

Stage 2 resolves each coding through cm.fhir_system_to_omop_vocab + Maps-to ONCE in mapspec/etl/_resolve_diagnosticreport.sql, dedups when two codings of the same report resolve to the same standard concept_id, and routes by std.domain_id. The sibling stage-2 SQLs are then trivial `WHERE std_domain='…'` filters over staging.diagnosticreport_resolved — same shape as the Condition and Observation families.

The DR's free-text body (presentedForm / conclusion) is handled separately by the DiagnosticReport__note edge, which is per-report (not per-coding) and keeps its own view.

column.name: id
column.description: DR.id; → surrogate via stringToId(id || '|' || std_concept_id) in stage 2 (fan-out safe).

column.name: performer_practitioner_ref
column.description: First Practitioner-typed performer → provider_id. Organization performers (labs) are intentionally not written — OMOP measurement/observation/procedure have no care_site_id, and the lab is already implicit via the visit's care_site.
